inline_comments.comment: `tagsValue ?? null` should be `tagsValue || null`. `serializeTagsParam` returns an empty string `''` (not `null`/`undefined`) when both filter arrays are empty (see tags.ts line 40-42). The nullish coalescing operator `??` only replaces `null`/`undefined`, so `{ tags: '' }` is returned instead of `{ tags: null }`. This empty-string value then propagates through `api.applyQueryParams` → `navigateTo` and `setQueryParams`. In `navigateTo` (url.ts line 228), the filter is `v !== null && v !== undefined`, which passes empty strings, so `tags=` (empty value) gets appended to the URL when all filters are cleared. Simultaneously, `setQueryParams({ tags: '' })` stores `tags: ''` into `customQueryParams` (since `''` is not null/undefined, the delete branch is not taken), causing the stale key to persist in subsequent `navigateWithQueryParams` calls. Use `tagsValue || null` to correctly coerce the empty string to `null`.

`storyPassesFilter` only checks for `type === 'story'`, which means any `docs` entry in `filteredIndex` is treated as if it did not pass the filter. If the user navigates directly to a docs URL (e.g., `/docs/my-component--docs?tags=play`) and that docs entry IS present in `filteredIndex`, `storyPassesFilter` evaluates to `false`, triggering an unnecessary redirect away from the valid entry the user explicitly requested. The check should mirror the search on line 969-972 and accept either `'story'` or `'docs'`: `filteredIndex && (filteredIndex[storyId]?.type === 'story' || filteredIndex[storyId]?.type === 'docs')`.

When redirecting to `firstFiltered`, the path is built as `/${viewMode}/${firstFiltered}`, where `viewMode` comes from the `STORY_SPECIFIED` event payload. However, `firstFiltered` can be a `docs` entry (lines 969-972 search for `entry.type === 'story' || entry.type === 'docs'`). If `viewMode` from the event is `'story'` (the common case) but `firstFiltered` is a docs entry, the navigation produces `/story/<docs-id>` — an incorrect path. The entry's own type should be used: `navigateWithQueryParams(`/${filteredIndex[firstFiltered].type}/${firstFiltered}`)`.

`selectFirstStory` with active filters searches only for `type === 'story'` entries in `filteredIndex`. If the filtered sidebar contains only docs entries (e.g., when the user has tagged a component-level docs page and no story matches), `firstStory` will be `undefined`, the method silently returns without navigation, and the user sees no selection. This is inconsistent with the `STORY_SPECIFIED` handler (lines 969-972) which considers both `'story'` and `'docs'`. The search predicate here should also accept `type === 'docs'` as a fallback.

`navigateWithQueryParams` replicates the parameter filtering, sorting, and URL construction logic already present in `navigateTo` (url.ts lines 227-232). If the serialisation convention (sort order, separator, encoding) ever changes in one place, the other silently diverges. Consider exposing a navigation helper through the module API (or accepting the navigate function from the URL module's already-built `navigateTo`) so there is a single source of truth for URL construction.

general_comments.comment: The new `hasActiveFilters && !stateHasSelection` branch in the `STORY_SPECIFIED` handler (around line 964) depends on `filteredIndex` being populated before the event fires. If `STORY_SPECIFIED` arrives before the index has been processed and `filteredIndex` is still `null`/`undefined`, `firstFiltered` will be `undefined`, the redirect is silently skipped, and the user lands with no story selected despite active filters. There is no retry or deferred-selection mechanism to recover from this race. A comment at minimum, and ideally a guard that re-runs the selection logic once `filteredIndex` is ready, would make the behavior explicit.
